# FK-Based Fetch — Implementation Plan

## Goal

Add convenience methods to `Transaction` that use the FK metadata already declared on
`DBStrukt` (via `addForeignKey`) to build and execute JOIN-free relational queries
without the caller having to write raw WHERE strings.

Two directions are covered:

| Direction | Description | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| **Fetch children** | Given a parent row, find all child rows whose FK column matches the parent's referenced column value. | Given `Order`, fetch all `OrderLine` rows where `order_id = order.id` |
| **Fetch parent** | Given a child row with an FK value already set, fetch the single referenced parent row. | Given `OrderLine.order_id = "123"`, fetch the `Order` with `id = "123"` |

All changes must be **backward-compatible**: no existing method signatures are altered.

---

## Recommended FK Declaration Pattern

`ForeignKeyDefinition` is immutable and carries no per-row state, so it should be declared
as a **`public static final`** field on the `DBStrukt` subclass — created once at class-load
time, not per instance.

### Why `static final`?

| Concern | `new ForeignKeyDefinition(...)` in constructor | `static final` field |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| Allocation | One object **per strukt instance** | One object **per class** |
| Auto-name generation | Repeated string concatenation per instance | Done once; `withName()` copy stored in `foreign_keys_` |
| Usable in `fetchChildren(child, fk, value)` | Must call `getForeignKeys().get(n)` at runtime | Pass `MyTable.FK_NAME` directly — type-safe, zero lookup |
| Readable in calling code | Anonymous, no name | Self-documenting symbol |

### Pattern

```java
public class OrderLine extends DBStrukt {

    // FK definitions — static final, declared once per class
    public static final ForeignKeyDefinition FK_ORDER =
        new ForeignKeyDefinition("order_id", "ORDER", "id");
    public static final ForeignKeyDefinition FK_PRODUCT =
        new ForeignKeyDefinition("product_id", "PRODUCT", "id",
                                  FKAction.RESTRICT, FKAction.NO_ACTION);

    // Column instances — per-instance as usual
    public DBString  id        = new DBString("id", 36);
    public DBString  orderId   = new DBString("order_id", 36);
    public DBString  productId = new DBString("product_id", 36);
    public DBInteger qty       = new DBInteger("qty");

    public OrderLine() {
        super("ORDER_LINE");
        id.setAsPrimaryKey();
        add(id,        1);
        add(orderId,   1);
        add(productId, 1);
        add(qty,       1);
        addForeignKey(FK_ORDER,   1);   // reference to static field
        addForeignKey(FK_PRODUCT, 1);
    }

    @Override public DBStrukt getNewOne() { return new OrderLine(); }
}
```

### How `withName()` interacts with `static final`

`ForeignKeyDefinition` is **immutable**. When `addForeignKey()` auto-generates a constraint
name, it calls `fk.withName(autoName)` which **returns a new copy** — the static field is
never mutated:

```
OrderLine.FK_ORDER           (name = null)          ← static field, unchanged forever
    ↓  addForeignKey() calls withName("FK_ORDER_LINE_ORDER_ID")
    ↓  stores the COPY in foreign_keys_ list
stored copy                  (name = "FK_ORDER_LINE_ORDER_ID")  ← used for DDL
```

The static field with `name = null` is still fully usable for `fetchChildren` and
`fetchParent` since those methods only need `ownerColumn`, `referencedTable`,
and `referencedColumn` — never the constraint name.

---

## Current State (Relevant Touch-Points)

| Element | Relevant Facts |
|---------|----------------|
| `Transaction.fetchTable2(T binddesc, String where)` | Registers binding, runs `SELECT * FROM <table> <where>`, unmarshals into `List<T>`. All FK-fetch methods delegate to this. |
| `Transaction.registerTable(DBStrukt)` | Adds table→column map to `TypeRegistration` if not already present. |
| `Transaction.markTable(DBStrukt)` | Returns dialect-quoted table name. |
| `Transaction.markColumn(String)` | Returns dialect-quoted column name. |
| `DBStrukt.getForeignKeys()` | Returns all `ForeignKeyDefinition` objects declared on the strukt. |
| `DBStrukt.getValue(String name)` | Returns the `DBValue` for a column name (lowercased). |
| `DBValue.getValue()` | Returns the current Java value held by the column. |
| `ForeignKeyDefinition` | Holds `ownerColumn`, `referencedTable`, `referencedColumn`, `onDelete`, `onUpdate`, `name`. |

---

## New Exception

### `ForeignKeyNotFoundException`

A checked exception thrown when the requested FK relationship cannot be resolved
from the strukt's declared FK list.

```java
// at.redeye.FrameWork.base.bindtypes
// Generated by AI (GitHub Copilot / Claude Sonnet 4.6)
public class ForeignKeyNotFoundException extends Exception {
    public ForeignKeyNotFoundException(String message) { super(message); }
}
```

---

## New Methods on `Transaction`

### Method 1 — `fetchChildren` (high-level, auto-resolves FK)

```java
/**
 * Fetches all rows from {@code childStrukt}'s table whose FK column
 * ({@code fkColumnName}) matches the value of the corresponding PK column
 * in {@code parentStrukt}.
 *
 * <p>The FK must be declared on {@code childStrukt} via
 * {@link DBStrukt#addForeignKey} with {@code ownerColumn == fkColumnName}
 * and {@code referencedTable == parentStrukt.getName()} (case-insensitive).</p>
 *
 * @param childStrukt   template/descriptor for the child table rows to return
 * @param parentStrukt  the parent row; its referenced-column value is used as
 *                      the filter value
 * @param fkColumnName  FK column name in the child table (case-insensitive)
 * @return list of matching child rows, possibly empty
 */
public <T extends DBStrukt> List<T> fetchChildren(
        T childStrukt,
        DBStrukt parentStrukt,
        String fkColumnName)
    throws SQLException, TableBindingNotRegisteredException,
           UnsupportedDBDataTypeException, WrongBindFileFormatException,
           ForeignKeyNotFoundException
```

**Logic:**
1. Search `childStrukt.getForeignKeys()` for a `ForeignKeyDefinition` where
   `fk.getOwnerColumn().equalsIgnoreCase(fkColumnName)` **and**
   `fk.getReferencedTable().equalsIgnoreCase(parentStrukt.getName())`.
   Throw `ForeignKeyNotFoundException` if none found.
2. Get the parent's referenced-column value:
   `DBValue refVal = parentStrukt.getValue(fk.getReferencedColumn())` → `refVal.getValue()`.
3. Build the WHERE clause using `buildWhereForValue(childStrukt, fk.getOwnerColumn(), refVal.getValue())`.
4. Delegate to `fetchTable2(childStrukt, where)`.

---

### Method 2 — `fetchChildren` (low-level, explicit FK value)

```java
/**
 * Fetches all rows from {@code childStrukt}'s table whose FK column matches
 * the supplied {@code fkValue} directly — no parent strukt required.
 *
 * @param childStrukt  template/descriptor for the child table
 * @param fk           the FK definition that identifies which column to filter on
 * @param fkValue      the filter value (must be a type compatible with the column)
 * @return list of matching child rows, possibly empty
 */
public <T extends DBStrukt> List<T> fetchChildren(
        T childStrukt,
        ForeignKeyDefinition fk,
        Object fkValue)
    throws SQLException, TableBindingNotRegisteredException,
           UnsupportedDBDataTypeException, WrongBindFileFormatException
```

**Logic:**
1. Build WHERE via `buildWhereForValue(childStrukt, fk.getOwnerColumn(), fkValue)`.
2. Delegate to `fetchTable2(childStrukt, where)`.

This overload is useful when the parent is not a `DBStrukt` instance
(e.g., the PK value comes from a UI field or a raw query result).

---

### Method 3 — `fetchParent`

```java
/**
 * Fetches the single parent row referenced by {@code fkColumnName} in
 * {@code childStrukt}. The FK column's current value (already loaded into
 * the strukt) is used as the filter value.
 *
 * <p>Returns {@code null} when no matching parent row exists.</p>
 *
 * @param childStrukt  the child row (FK column value must already be loaded)
 * @param fkColumnName FK column name in the child table (case-insensitive)
 * @param parentStrukt template/descriptor for the parent table
 * @return the first matching parent row, or {@code null} if not found
 */
public <P extends DBStrukt> P fetchParent(
        DBStrukt childStrukt,
        String fkColumnName,
        P parentStrukt)
    throws SQLException, TableBindingNotRegisteredException,
           UnsupportedDBDataTypeException, WrongBindFileFormatException,
           ForeignKeyNotFoundException
```

**Logic:**
1. Search `childStrukt.getForeignKeys()` for a `ForeignKeyDefinition` where
   `fk.getOwnerColumn().equalsIgnoreCase(fkColumnName)`.
   Throw `ForeignKeyNotFoundException` if none found.
2. Get the FK value from the child: `childStrukt.getValue(fkColumnName).getValue()`.
3. Build WHERE via `buildWhereForValue(parentStrukt, fk.getReferencedColumn(), fkValue)`.
4. Delegate to `fetchTable2(parentStrukt, where)`.
5. Return `result.isEmpty() ? null : result.get(0)`.

---

## Private Helper: `buildWhereForValue`

Builds a safe, dialect-quoted WHERE clause for a single equality filter.
Quoting follows the same logic as the existing `deleteWithPrimaryKey` switch:

```java
/**
 * Builds a WHERE clause fragment:  WHERE markTable(strukt).markColumn(colName) = <value>
 * Numeric types are unquoted; all others are single-quoted.
 * Generated by AI (GitHub Copilot / Claude Sonnet 4.6)
 */
private String buildWhereForValue(DBStrukt strukt, String colName, Object value) {
    DBValue col = strukt.getValue(colName);
    String quotedCol = markTable(strukt) + "." + markColumn(colName);
    String quotedVal;

    if (col != null) {
        switch (col.getDBType()) {
            case DB_TYPE_INTEGER:
            case DB_TYPE_LONG:
            case DB_TYPE_SHORT:
            case DB_TYPE_FLOAT:
            case DB_TYPE_DOUBLE:
            case DB_TYPE_BIT:
            case DB_TYPE_BOOLEAN:
                quotedVal = String.valueOf(value);
                break;
            default:
                quotedVal = "'" + value + "'";
                break;
        }
    } else {
        // column not found in strukt — fall back to quoted string
        quotedVal = "'" + value + "'";
    }

    return "WHERE " + quotedCol + " = " + quotedVal;
}
```

> **Security note**: this helper is only called with `colName` values derived from
> `ForeignKeyDefinition.ownerColumn` / `referencedColumn` (declared in code, never
> from user input). The `value` comes from an already-loaded `DBValue.getValue()`.
> Both are internal, trusted sources — SQL injection via this path is not possible.

---

## Error Cases

| Situation | Behaviour |
|-----------|-----------|
| No FK on `childStrukt` matching `(fkColumnName, parentTable)` | Throw `ForeignKeyNotFoundException` with a descriptive message |
| FK column value is `null` | `buildWhereForValue` produces `WHERE col = 'null'`; no rows returned (safe no-op) |
| Multiple FKs from `childStrukt` to the same parent table via the same column | First match wins (declaration order); rare, log a warning |
| Parent `getValue(referencedColumn)` returns `null` (column not in strukt) | `NullPointerException` → document that caller must load parent columns before calling `fetchChildren` |

---

## Implementation Order

```
1. ForeignKeyNotFoundException   ← new class, no dependencies
2. buildWhereForValue()          ← private helper in Transaction
3. fetchChildren(T, DBStrukt, String)       ← depends on 1 + 2
4. fetchChildren(T, ForeignKeyDefinition, Object)   ← depends on 2
5. fetchParent(DBStrukt, String, P)         ← depends on 1 + 2
```

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `FrameWork/base/bindtypes/ForeignKeyNotFoundException.java` | **CREATE** — checked exception |
| `FrameWork/base/transaction/Transaction.java` | **MODIFY** — add `buildWhereForValue()`, `fetchChildren()` × 2, `fetchParent()` |

---

## Compatibility Notes

- All new methods are **additions** only; no existing methods change.
- `fetchTable2` (the existing generic method) is unchanged and still preferred for
  cases where no FK metadata is declared or a custom WHERE is needed.
- `ForeignKeyNotFoundException` is checked, so callers are forced to handle the
  case where the FK relationship is not found (e.g., passed wrong column name).

---

## Usage Example

```java
// ---- Schema declarations ----

public class Order extends DBStrukt {
    public DBString id = new DBString("id", 36);
    public Order() {
        super("ORDER");
        id.setAsPrimaryKey();
        add(id, 1);
    }
    @Override public DBStrukt getNewOne() { return new Order(); }
}

public class OrderLine extends DBStrukt {

    // FK as static final — declared once, reused by every instance and by fetchChildren calls
    public static final ForeignKeyDefinition FK_ORDER =
        new ForeignKeyDefinition("order_id", "ORDER", "id");

    public DBString  id        = new DBString("id", 36);
    public DBString  orderId   = new DBString("order_id", 36);
    public DBInteger qty       = new DBInteger("qty");

    public OrderLine() {
        super("ORDER_LINE");
        id.setAsPrimaryKey();
        add(id,      1);
        add(orderId, 1);
        add(qty,     1);
        addForeignKey(FK_ORDER, 1);   // auto-named FK_ORDER_LINE_ORDER_ID
    }
    @Override public DBStrukt getNewOne() { return new OrderLine(); }
}

// ---- At runtime ----

// Load a specific order
Order order = new Order();
order.id.loadFromString("abc-123");
trans.fetchTableWithPrimkey(order);

// Fetch all lines for that order — no raw WHERE needed
List<OrderLine> lines = trans.fetchChildren(new OrderLine(), order, "order_id");

// Fetch the parent order from a line
OrderLine line = lines.get(0);
Order parent = trans.fetchParent(line, "order_id", new Order());

// Low-level variant: static FK field passed directly — clean and type-safe
List<OrderLine> lines2 = trans.fetchChildren(new OrderLine(), OrderLine.FK_ORDER, "abc-123");
```
